human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for grocery inventory boundary discipline, source quality, availability and replenishment evidence handling, vendor and substitution authority controls, traceability, recall and food-safety escalation, fraud and customer-impact routing, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

source_classes_covered: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Public procurement, supply-chain, inventory, or vendor-management guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

deployment_readiness.runtime_claim: specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to grocery stock, availability, replenishment, vendor constraint, receiving, traceability, shortage, and customer-impact inventory work.
Authority sources cover FTC order and fraud guidance, NIST price and unit-pricing standards, FDA Food Code, FDA and eCFR traceability rules, FDA and FSIS recalls, and GS1 retail grocery standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover shortages, vendor allocation, substitution authority, release-control refusal, traceability gaps, pricing/refund/safety escalation, high-volume pick exceptions, adjacent lane rejection, low-confidence missing facts, fraud and customer-harm signals, conflicting policies, tenant adaptation, and grocery terminology.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/grocery-and-food-retail/grocery-inventory-specialist/ so inventory availability remains separate from pricing, guest services, fraud, food safety, category management, procurement approval, and operations execution lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around spend approval, supplier substitution, pricing override, refund approval, safety release, recall disposition, fraud adjudication, and legal interpretation.

regulated_lane_caveats: Food-safety, traceability, recall, SNAP/EBT, pricing, refund, privacy, and fraud rules vary by jurisdiction and tenant model.
The spec pack does not grant legal, safety, financial, supplier, or policy override authority.
orchestrator_return_conditions: required facts or authoritative records are missing
public and tenant sources conflict materially
approval, release, refund, fraud, legal, or safety authority determines the outcome

notes: This package is suitable as a validated grocery and food retail inventory specialist that supports evidence-backed availability, replenishment, vendor-risk, shortage, traceability, and customer-impact escalation workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
